notes: Headline: 13% relative employment decline for early-career workers in most-exposed occupations even controlling for firm-level shocks
Software developers age 22-25: down nearly 20% from late-2022 peak. Sharpest single-occupation impact
Same-occupation employment for workers over 35 ROSE — pattern is age-specific, not occupation-only. Apprenticeship-ladder break
Effect concentrated in occupations where AI usage is automative per Anthropic classification, not augmentative. Encodes the augmentation-vs-automation distinction
Generative AI exposed freelancer occupations: 2% decline in monthly jobs
5.2% decline in monthly earnings for affected freelancer occupations
Image-related workers post-DALL-E/Midjourney: 3.7% contract decline + 9.4% income loss
Counter-intuitive: top performers hit hardest — 0.5% additional drop per 1% past earnings
Around 80% of US workforce has at least 10% of tasks exposed to LLMs
Around 19% of workers may see at least 50% of tasks impacted
With complementary software jumps to 46% of jobs
